provenance.source_corpus: How to shift unproductive Kalmia angustifolia – Rhododendron groenlandicum heath to productive conifer plantation

source_spans.text: We evaluated scarification, spot fertilization, and increased seedling foliar N concentration as treatments to promote planted black spruce ( Picea mariana (Mill.) BSP) seedling survival and growth.
Seedling heights were greater in the scarified plots (S1 and S2) than in the unscarified ones (S0); this difference increased with time
The positive effect of scarification on GLD and biomass was enhanced when seedlings were fertilized
From early June to early September of each year, the soil temperature was systematically higher in the S1 and S2 plots than in the S0 plots

Scarification was associated with the emergence of species that were not observed in control plots; the treatments favoured the emergence of intolerant, early-successional species and significantly reduced the abundance of ericaceous shrubs.
After three growing seasons (October 2002) fertilization had a significant adverse effect on seedling survival
Fertilization at time of planting decreased fifth-year foliar N concentration by 5% compared with F0
Fertilization, either F1 or F2, significantly reduced the foliar P concentration in N1 seedlings compared with F0
In 2001, P concentrations were 30% lower in scarified plots than in control plots
Scarification, either single- or double-pass, significantly enhanced soil extractable Ca concentrations in 2003
We evaluated scarification, spot fertilization, and increased seedling foliar N concentration as treatments to promote planted black spruce ( Picea mariana (Mill.) BSP) seedling survival and growth.
We evaluated scarification, spot fertilization, and increased seedling foliar N concentration as treatments to promote planted black spruce ( Picea mariana (Mill.) BSP) seedling survival and growth.
double-pass scarification proved slightly more effective than a single-pass treatment
double-pass scarification proved slightly more effective than a single-pass treatment
Forest Pak fertilization (F1) was associated with 3% mortality
seedlings fertilized with Silva Pak (F2) exhibited 13%
Kalmia angustifolia L. – Rhododendron groenlandicum (Oeder) K.A. Kron & Judd cover
